results.possibleIdentity: John S. Almen (John Sigfrid Almen, 1927–1988) was an electrical and audio-video engineer at Ampex Corporation in Redwood City, California. Active in the late 1960s and 1970s, he was a key figure in the regional activities of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit founded to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. In 1968, he was identified as the head of the E.A.T. local group for the Redwood City and San Francisco area, where he facilitated technical support and partnerships for artists. Professionally, Almen specialized in video tape recording, acoustics, and television systems. He participated in industry events such as the 1974 Broadcast Industry Conference at San Francisco State University and contributed technical expertise to research in video microscopy, as cited in Shinya Inoué's 1986 work 'Video Microscopy.' A resident of Belmont and San Mateo, California, he was married to Lois Everett, an arts advocate who later made memorial donations in his name to the Mission Hospice. He died on February 13, 1988.
